name: Heap Fundamentals — Min/Max Heap, PriorityQueue API

metadata.career_relevance: PriorityQueue is the most-used 'black box' in Java DSA. Top K problems are in 40% of medium interviews. Heap sort is O(n log n) O(1) space — asked at Flipkart/Amazon as a space-efficient alternative to merge sort.

hook.what_you_will_build: Heap Fundamentals — Min/Max Heap, PriorityQueue API

key_takeaways: PriorityQueue: min-heap by default. Max-heap: Collections.reverseOrder() or (a,b)->b-a
offer(x): O(log n). poll(): O(log n) — removes minimum. peek(): O(1)
Heap sort: heapify O(n) + n extractions O(n log n) = O(n log n) O(1) space
Java PriorityQueue does NOT support O(1) contains or arbitrary removal
Initialization from collection: new PriorityQueue<>(list) — O(n) heapify
reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: Heap Patterns — K largest, K closest, median stream.
